mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side efficiency, which supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates labor market tightening and wage growth, boosting consumer demand and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations or interaction IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue resilience, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when monetary policy remains accommodative.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or signal accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent upward momentum in industrial production signals healthy supply chain throughput and manufacturing demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.
